title: Generation criteria for the Fukaya category II
speaker: Mohammed Abouzaid

proof_stages.label: Ribbon-graph model for curves
proof_stages.summary: For an open Riemann surface with ribbon-graph skeleton, the speaker constructs the local cosheaf of A-infinity categories and checks that its Hochschild-homology fundamental class maps to the relative fundamental class, so the generation criterion sees all compact Lagrangians.

proof_stages.label: Smooth cotangent-bundle base case
proof_stages.summary: When Q is a smooth compact manifold, triangulation gives a constant cosheaf with one-object stalk, recovering the standard cotangent-fiber description and the computation of compact Fukaya data by modules over cochains on Q in the simply connected case.

proof_stages.label: Boundary-glued singular bases and Liouville manifolds
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines singular spaces obtained by gluing manifolds Q_i along boundary components over P with a locally constant cyclic ordering, then constructs the corresponding Liouville manifold by gluing T*Q_i to T*P times a planar disk with spokes.

proof_stages.label: Local product computation of singular stalks
proof_stages.summary: Near a singular point of P the geometry is a product of a cotangent fiber in T*P with the one-dimensional spoke model, so projection and a Hochschild-cohomology deformation argument identify the singular stalk with the same cyclic A-infinity category as in the curve case.

proof_stages.label: Generation for boundary plumbings
proof_stages.summary: The generalized boundary-plumbing cosheaf is asserted to satisfy the generation hypothesis, allowing Floer cohomology of compact Lagrangians in these Liouville manifolds to be computed from the associated modules over the cosheaf.

proof_stages.label: Classical cotangent plumbing and naive obstruction
proof_stages.summary: Classical plumbing of T*N1 and T*N2 along a common submanifold B is recalled via the normal-bundle involution, and the direct attempt to use the naive skeleton N1 union_B N2 is shown to fail in codimension greater than one because the cotangent-fiber family does not extend over B.

proof_stages.label: Trivalent skeleton for ordinary plumbings
proof_stages.summary: The ordinary plumbing is given an alternative skeleton with three smooth components—N1 minus a neighborhood of B, N2 minus a neighborhood of B, and the disk bundle of the common normal bundle—glued along their common boundary with cyclic order, putting it into the boundary-plumbing framework.

proof_stages.label: A2 Milnor-space Lagrangian application
proof_stages.summary: As an application, a joint theorem with Ivan Smith is stated for the A2 Milnor space T*S^n plumbed with T*S^n at a point: closed exact Maslov-zero Lagrangians are Fukaya-equivalent to iterated Dehn twists of a zero section, hence are homology spheres.
